text: When setting project goals, it’s important to make them SMART: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase website traffic by 20% in three months.” Breaking the project into milestones helps track progress. Each milestone represents a key step, like completing the design phase. Deliverables are the tangible outcomes, such as a final report or a prototype. Finally, stakeholder alignment ensures everyone agrees on the goals and expectations. This avoids misunderstandings later. By using these strategies, your project is more likely to succeed on time and within budget.
